basics.summary: A German-born theoretical physicist, widely ranked among the greatest and most influential scientists of all time

education.studyType: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master in Intelligent Field Robotics System
education.area: Universitat de Girona and University of Zagreb

education.studyType: Bachelor of Control Engineering and Automation
education.area: Hanoi University of Science and Technology, Vietnam
courses: Relevant Courses: Control Theory, Robust and Optimal Control, Fuzzy Control and Neural Networks, Robot Engineering, Logic Control and PLC,  Microprocessors, Embedded Control System Design, Probability and Statistics, Electrical Circuit

work.name: Viettel High Technology Industries Corporation
work.summary: Led an autopilot team of 5 members, Responsible for technical decisions and team communication, Designed software system of the autopilot.

work.name: Viettel Aerospace Institute
work.summary: Designed and developed a navigation system for UAV, Tested and calibrated sensors: IMU, Barometers, GNSS, Magnetometer.

work.name: Hanoi University of Science and Technology
work.summary: Researched new algorithms and control theory in autonomous vehicles, Published 5 papers on journals and conferences.

volunteer.organization: CTES Volunteer Team, Hanoi University of Science and Technology
volunteer.summary: participated in volunteer activities supporting food and clothes for homeless people (2015-2016), organised Mid-Autumn festival for children in Viet Tri city, Phu Tho province (2015), organised Tet holiday for people in Ba Vi district, Hanoi (2016).
highlights: Awarded 'CTES Award' award by CTES Volunteer Team leader for my efforts organizing.

volunteer.organization: National Institute of Haematology and Blood Transfusion

volunteer.summary: a high school student volunteer team with contribution to society, life and study of students at Hai Duong province.

awards.title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's Degree (EMJMD) Scholarship
awards.summary: The Erasmus Mundus Intelligent Field Robotic Systems (IFRoS) programme is a 2-year joint master's degree with 120 ECTS coordinated by the Universitat de Girona (UdG, Spain) together with the University of Zagreb (UNIZG, Croatia) and the Eötvös Loránd University (ELTE, Hungary).

publications.name: A Finite-Time Sliding Mode Controller Design for Flexible Joint Manipulator Systems based on Disturbance Observer
publications.publisher: International Journal of Mechanical Engineering and Robotics Research
publications.summary: This paper presents an external disturbance observer (DO) for flexible joint single-link manipulator. Moreover, the arbitrary small attraction region is obtained by using the suitable parameters. The main result of this paper is proposed based on theoretical analysis of differential equations without any traditional Lyapunov stability analysis.

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
